name: Limit POP
description: The Limit POP takes any attribute and lets you clamp to a lower-upper range, or takes values outside the range and loops them so they are withing the range, or similarly zig-zags the values within the range. You can limit the maximum-only, or the minimum-only. If the attribute is multi-component (like Color which has 4 components), then by default all components have the limits applied in the same way. But if you change Parameter Size to, say, 3, then there are a set of parameters for each color component. So R, G and B can have different Limit Types and different minimum and maximum ranges. Some of these capabilities are possible in the [[Math Mix POP]] and [[Math Combine POP]] using the min(A), max(A), loop(), zigzag() and clamp() functions. Values can then be made all positive, and then the final values can be cast to integer attributes (integer, unsigned integer). '''Tip''': If you want to only work with red and blue color components, set Unput Attribute Scope to Color(0) Color(2), or Color.rb and set Parameter Size to 2 for independent control. See also [[Quantize POP]], [[Math Mix POP]], [[Math Combine POP]]

parameters.name: Attribute Class
parameters.label: Attribute Class
parameters.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this POP to act only upon the group specified.

parameters.name: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Input's attributes you want to affect within the chosen attribute class, or attribute components.

parameters.name: Parameter Size
parameters.label: Parameter Size
parameters.description: Number of independent configurable parameter values.

parameters.name: Limit Type Minimum
parameters.label: Limit Type Minimum
parameters.description: The wrapping method used when applying limits to the input elements attribute.

parameters.name: Limit Type Maximum
parameters.label: Limit Type Maximum
parameters.description: The wrapping method used when applying limits to the input elements attribute.

parameters.name: Minimum Value
parameters.label: Minimum Value
parameters.description: The minimum value that the selected attribute can have in the output.

parameters.name: Maximum Value
parameters.label: Maximum Value
parameters.description: The maximum value that the selected attribute can have in the output.

parameters.name: Cast to
parameters.label: Cast to
parameters.description: Allows to cast the output attribute to a different type if wanted.

parameters.name: Output Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Output Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Name of attribute to output (can choose components of attribute), can choose from menu.

parameters.name: Quantize
parameters.label: Quantize
parameters.description: Convert values into a finite set of discrete levels.

parameters.name: Value Step
parameters.label: Value Step
parameters.description: Determines the step value used in quantization.

parameters.name: Value Offset
parameters.label: Value Offset
parameters.description: Determines the offset value used in quantization.
